title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. subampliatum
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. subampliatum, also known as a subalpine hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family and is native to the European Alps. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. subampliatum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
